Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for ENG-212 using linear_list_issues (query: "edge case" or "ENG-212" or "test coverage", or similar) to find issue_edge_cases_backlog (number 212, assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov, state_in_progress, project proj_expense_autocategorizer). Read the ticket description to discover the 6 existing test categories: malformed policy config JSON, exclusion list with unexpected data type, generated policy configs match source data, category overlap (ergonomic vs furniture), bundled item receipt descriptions, and receipt descriptions with vendor abbreviations.
OE 2: List or search Airtable Stipend Transactions records using airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions) or airtable_search_records to find all pilot client stipend records. Discover 8 incorrect expense decisions across both pilot clients: 5 false rejections for Vectral Systems (Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet, Maria Santos $550 desk, James Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 WiFi) and 3 false approvals for Mosaic Robotics (Andre Washington $1,200 desk, Andre Washington $350 coworking, Kenji Nakamura $135 coworking passes). From the review notes on each record, trace which component broke: all 5 Vectral false rejections caused by PolicyValidator using stale default limits (ENG-214), all 3 Mosaic false approvals caused by PolicyValidator with malformed JSON types (ENG-215). Critically, James Okafor's keyboard tray (recStip000000019) had a double failure: CategoryMatcher miscategorized it as office_supplies instead of ergonomic AND PolicyValidator applied the stale $50 default limit. Also discover that some expenses have duplicate Airtable records under different IDs (rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk with status "Pending Approval" alongside recStip000000018 with status "Rejected" for the same Maria Santos desk, and rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi with status "Pending Approval" alongside recStip000000020 with status "Rejected" for the same Lisa Park WiFi).
OE 3: Search Slack for context on the ExpenseBot pilot failures using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "ExpenseBot" or "pilot" or "auto-categorizer" or similar) to find engineering discussions in channel C003 (#engineering) about the pilot launch, incident discovery, and remediation timeline.
OE 4: Search Slack for context on specific failure types using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "false rejection" or "Mosaic config" or "Vectral config" or "excluded_categories" or "stale default" or similar) to find technical discussions about the specific config bugs, malformed JSON types, and the string comparison issues that caused the Mosaic false approvals.
OE 5: Search emails for context on the pilot failures using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "ExpenseBot" or "audit" or similar) to find Dmitri's audit findings email to Marcus detailing the 3 flagged TechComfort receipts, Marcus's response about placing financial holds on STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097, Samira's remediation plan email, and the audit summary email listing all 8 incorrect decisions.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for stipend-related bills using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true, or with criteria related to stipend) to cross-check the financial records against Airtable amounts. Discover 5 stipend bills in QuickBooks, all related to the duplicate receipt cluster: Kevin Tran keyboard bill STIP-2026-0089 ($89, Balance $0, already paid), Andre Washington keyboard bill STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Balance $89, pending), Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand bill STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Balance $45, pending), plus ADMIN HOLD versions of the Washington and Ekwueme bills placed by Marcus Thorne. Note that QuickBooks does NOT contain bills for the false rejection or false approval amounts -- those financial figures come solely from the Airtable stipend records.
OE 7: Search Linear for Lena's fix tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Vectral config" or "Mosaic config" or "pipeline hardening" or assignee: moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, or similar) to find ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config, state_in_progress), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, state_in_progress), and ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening, state_in_progress). All three are still showing in_progress. Cross-check that these tickets address the root causes found in the stipend records.
OE 8: Search Linear for existing tickets related to CategoryMatcher keyword scoring using linear_list_issues (query: "CategoryMatcher" or "keyword" or "classification" or similar) to determine whether the James Okafor miscategorization bug (keyboard tray classified as office_supplies instead of ergonomic) has a remediation ticket. Discover ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher) which is the original CategoryMatcher implementation ticket, but NO ticket specifically addresses the keyword scoring gap that caused the miscategorization. ENG-214 covers config limits, ENG-215 covers JSON types, ENG-217 covers pipeline hardening -- none address the CategoryMatcher keyword gap.
OE 9: Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne, Samira Tariq, and Lena Bjorkstrom using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus" or "Marcus Thorne", then "Samira" or "Samira Tariq", then "Lena" or "Lena Bjorkstrom") to retrieve their email addresses: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com.
OE 10: Look up the #engineering Slack channel ID using channels_list (channel_types: public_channel) to find channel C003.
OE 11: Add test cases as separate comments on ENG-212 using linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_edge_cases_backlog, body: test case content). Each comment should contain test cases derived from real production failure data, organized by component -- covering CategoryMatcher keyword gaps (keyboard tray miscategorized as office_supplies), PolicyValidator type coercion bugs (max_amount as string, excluded_categories as string), PolicyValidator stale config limits (all 5 Vectral amounts against correct limits), config ingestion pipeline failures (merged cell parse error causing silent fallback), and cross-component integration tests including the James Okafor double-fault scenario. Multiple separate linear_create_comment calls are required per the prompt instruction.
OE 12: Create a new Linear ticket for the CategoryMatcher classification bug using linear_create_issue (team: team_engineering, title referencing CategoryMatcher keyword gap or ergonomic miscategorization, description covering the James Okafor keyboard tray incident where "Ergonomic Keyboard Tray" was classified as office_supplies instead of ergonomic, with fix requirements for keyword scoring improvements). This is the failure pattern with no existing remediation ticket.
OE 13: Fix the Airtable stipend records still stuck in wrong states using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001). Update the 3 recStip records still showing Rejected: Maria Santos recStip000000018 from Rejected to Approved at $550, James Okafor recStip000000019 from Rejected to Approved at $82, and Lisa Park recStip000000020 from Rejected to Approved at $130. Also update the duplicate rec_stipend records that show incorrect statuses: rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk (currently Pending Approval, should be Approved at $550) and rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi (currently Pending Approval, should be Approved at $130). Each should include updated Review Notes documenting the correction.
OE 14: Send an email to Marcus Thorne using send_email (sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipients: marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with the financial impact breakdown by failure type. Content should cover: false rejections total ($1,422 across 5 Vectral employees with 3 corrected today), false approvals total ($885 absorbed by MoveOps across 3 Mosaic expenses), duplicate receipt exposure ($223 with $89 already disbursed and $134 on admin hold in QuickBooks), and the 3 records corrected today that need payment processing ($762 total for Santos, Okafor, Park).
OE 15: Send an email to Samira Tariq using send_email (sender: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, recipients: samira.tariq@moveops.com) with the test coverage gap analysis. Content should cover: which ENG-212 test categories map to which production failures, which gaps were found (CategoryMatcher keyword tests, cross-component integration tests, duplicate detection tests), what new test cases were added, and what new tickets were filed.
OE 16: Post a summary in the #engineering channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003, payload: summary content). Content should cover the production failure breakdown by component, financial impact, records corrected, test cases added to ENG-212, new tickets created, and the status of Lena's fix tickets (ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217 all still in_progress).
OE 17: Schedule a meeting with Lena using calendar_add_calendar_event (title referencing ENG-212 or test case review, attendees: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com and lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, with a start_datetime and end_datetime for an upcoming slot). The meeting should be for reviewing the new test cases against Lena's pipeline fixes and discussing the CategoryMatcher ticket.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✓Run #2The linear_update_issue description for issue_edge_cases_backlog explicitly includes INT-1: 'Double-fault: miscategorized + wrong limit | James Okafor keyboard tray' referencing both CategoryMatcher and PolicyValidator failing on the same expense.

✓Run #4The linear_update_issue description for issue_edge_cases_backlog explicitly references James Okafor's double-bug in Section C ('from James Okafor double-bug') and in the failure summary table which shows 'CategoryMatcher + PolicyValidator — False deny — DOUBLE BUG' for James Okafor's keyboard tray.
✓Run #5The linear_update_issue description explicitly references James Okafor's keyboard tray as a 'double bug' where CategoryMatcher miscategorized it as office_supplies AND PolicyValidator used stale limits, including a test case 'test_keyboard_tray_categorized_as_ergonomic' with the exact incident details.
✓Run #6The linear_update_issue description explicitly references James Okafor's $82 keyboard tray, mentions 'DOUBLE BUG,' CM-KW-001, and INT-004 describes 'James Okafor scenario end-to-end: "Ergonomic Keyboard Tray", $82 ... CategoryMatcher AND PolicyValidator both failed on the same expense.'
3 The test case content added to ENG-212 (visible in linear_create_comment body or linear_update_issue description parameters) references PolicyValidator type coercion bugs from Mosaic Robotics, specifically max_amount stored as string or excluded_categories stored as string instead of array (or similar).
outcome
